era: Daily liturgy in Sōtō, Rinzai, Plum Village, and many Mahāyāna halls
title: Kannon-gyō (観音経 — Universal Gate, chanted)
content:
## §1 Mu-jin-i bo-satsu mon-pō

*The Universal Gate of Avalokiteśvara chapter as chanted in Japanese Zen halls — the Sino-Japanese (on'yomi) reading of Kumārajīva's Chinese, written here in Hepburn romanisation. Known popularly as the* Kannon-gyō, *the most-recited Lotus chapter in Sōtō and Rinzai liturgy.*

Myō hō ren ge kyō, Kan-ze-on bo-satsu Fu-mon-bon dai ni jū go.

Ni-ji Mu-jin-i bo-satsu, soku jū za ki, hen tan u ken, gas-shō kō butsu ni saku ze gon: Se-son, Kan-ze-on bo-satsu, i ga in nen myō Kan-ze-on?

Butsu go Mu-jin-i bo-satsu: Zen-nan-shi, nyaku u mu-ryō hyaku sen man oku shu-jō, ju sho ku-nō, mon ze Kan-ze-on bo-satsu, is-shin shō myō, Kan-ze-on bo-satsu, soku ji kan go on-jō, kai toku ge-das-shi.

## §2 Shichi-nan ge-datsu

Nyaku u ji ze Kan-ze-on bo-satsu myō sha, set-su nyū dai ka, ka fu nō shō, yu ze bo-satsu i-jin riki ko. Nyaku i dai sui sho hyō, shō go myō gō, soku toku sen sho.

Setsu fu u nin, nyaku u zai nyaku mu zai, chū kai ka sa, ken-kei go shin, shō Kan-ze-on bo-satsu myō sha, kai shitsu dan-e, soku toku ge-datsu.

## §3 Sanjū-ni ō-jin

Mu-jin-i, ze Kan-ze-on bo-satsu, jō-jū nyo ze ku-doku. I shu-ju gyō, yu sho koku-do, do-das-shu-jō. Ze ko nyo-tō, ō-tō is-shin ku-yō Kan-ze-on bo-satsu.

Ō i butsu shin toku do sha, soku gen butsu shin ni i sep-pō. Ō i byaku-shi-butsu shin toku do sha, soku gen byaku-shi-butsu shin ni i sep-pō. Ō i shō-mon shin toku do sha, soku gen shō-mon shin ni i sep-pō.

## §4 Jū-ge

Ni-ji Mu-jin-i bo-satsu i ge mon watsu:

*Myō-on Kan-ze-on,*
*Bon-non kai-chō-on,*
*Shō pi se-ken-non,*
*Ze ko shu jō nen.*

*Nen-nen mot-tu shō gi,*
*Kan-ze-on jō shō,*
*O ku-nō shi-yaku,*
*Nō i sa e-go.*

Butsu setsu ze Fu-mon-bon ji, shu chū hachi man shi sen shu-jō, kai hot-tu mu-tō-tō a-noku-ta-ra san-myaku san-bo-dai shin.
edition: Standard Zen liturgy chant book — public-domain phonetic transcription
